heading: Taxes by States
label: 1341 Taxes by States
text: The district courts shall not enjoin, suspend or restrain the assessment, levy or collection of any tax under State law where a plain, speedy and efficient remedy may be had in the courts of such State.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 41(1) (, par. 1, ; , ; , ; , ).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 2436 Stat. 1091May 14, 1934, ch. 283, § 148 Stat. 775Aug. 21, 1937, ch. 726, § 150 Stat. 738Apr. 20, 1940, ch. 11754 Stat. 143This section restates the last sentence of , U.S.C., 1940 ed.section 41(1) of title 28Other provisions of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., are incorporated in sections 1331, 1332, 1342, 1345, 1354, and 1359 of this title.section 41(1) of title 28Words “at law or in equity” before “in the courts of such State” were omitted as unnecessary.Words “civil action” were substituted for “suit” in view of Rule 2 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.Words “under State law” were substituted for “imposed by or pursuant to the laws of any State” for the same reason.
